title: lemurangis madagascariensis
intro: Lemurangis madagascariensis is a plant species native to Madagascar, classified as a shrub or small tree in the family Rubiaceae, characterized by its woody stem and elliptical leaves. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Lemurangis madagascariensis include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves and stems, which can be consumed as teas or used topically to treat various health conditions.
